error_chain: Kombu connection pool in kombu/connection.py exhausts available connections to RabbitMQ broker
New connection attempt fails with ConnectionResetError in kombu/transport/pyamqp.py
Celery worker catches the ConnectionResetError as a generic Exception in celery/worker/request.py
Celery autoretry_for=(Exception,) in celery/app/task.py matches ConnectionResetError and schedules retry
Retry attempts to publish message through kombu which needs a connection from the exhausted pool
Cycle repeats creating a retry storm until max_retries is reached across all affected tasks
trigger_conditions: autoretry_for=(Exception,) is too broad and catches broker-level ConnectionResetError alongside task exceptions
Kombu connection pool reaches configured limit due to connection leaks or high concurrency
RabbitMQ server hits max connection limit and resets new connection attempts
Celery retry mechanism does not classify exceptions as task-level vs transport-level before retrying

expected_answer.error_origin: Kombu connection pool exhaustion in kombu/connection.py when broker rejects new connections
expected_answer.retry_mechanism: Celery autoretry_for in celery/app/task.py treats all Exception subclasses equally without distinguishing transport errors
expected_answer.root_cause: The autoretry_for mechanism has no built-in exception classification to exclude broker/transport errors from retry eligibility
